title: hessian OVER a forward derivative that captures the outer vector via vector-ref SIGSEGVs (even at depth 1)
goal: Found by the depth-parametric AD oracle (P6a), verified on -r AND AOT (both crash). Composing a HESSIAN (reverse-over-reverse) outer with an inner forward derivative that closes over the outer vector via vector-ref causes a SIGSEGV (fatal signal, exit 139) rather than a value — on both the JIT and AOT lanes. Minimal repro (tests/ad_depth/found/esh_0121_hessian_over_forward_sigsegv.esk): (hessian (lambda(v)(derivative (lambda(z)(* 0.5 (vref v 0)(vref v 0) z z z z z z z z)) 2.0)) (vector 3.0 4.0)) should give H[0][0]=1024 but segfaults. This is the hessian sibling of ESH-0120 (jacobian outer -> 0) and ESH-0117 (gradient outer over nested forward -> 0); the reverse-mode outer machinery does not survive an inner forward-derivative capturing the outer vector, and for hessian the failure is a memory fault rather than a silent zero. A crash is worse than a wrong value: it must at minimum degrade to a clean error.
detection_gap: P3 oracle's hessian cells never composed hessian with an inner AD op. P6a hessod.* cells (scripts/gen_ad_depth.py) sweep hessian-over-derivative^d, isolated one-per-file (ad_depth_hessod_xc_NN.esk) so the crash masks nothing; the runner classifies the whole file as LIMIT. Tracked by the ad-depth gate baseline (max-correct-depth 0).
acceptance: hessian-over-single-forward with vecref returns H[0][0]=1024 without crashing (-r AND AOT), OR degrades to a clean typed error; oracle hessod.* cells reach max-correct-depth >= 1 (or emit a clean LIMIT, never SIGSEGV); no regression to ad_oracle / ESH-0117 / ESH-0120
